papers.title: DVAO: Dynamic Variance-adaptive Advantage Optimization for Multi-reward Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Dynamic Variance-adaptive Advantage Optimization (DVAO) addresses training instability in multi-reward reinforcement learning by adaptively weighting objectives based on empirical reward variance, maintaining bounded advantage magnitudes and improving multi-objective performance.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement Learning has become a standard paradigm for aligning Large Language Models with human intent and task requirements. While Group Relative Policy Optimization offers an efficient, value-model-free alternative to Proximal Policy Optimization, adapting it to real-world multi-reward settings remains challenging. Standard scalarization practices, such as Reward Combination and Advantage Combination, suffer from significant drawbacks: Reward Combination frequently generates advantages with excessively large squared magnitudes that lead to training instability, while Advantage Combination relies on static hyperparameters and ignores cross-objective correlations. To address these limitations, we propose Dynamic Variance-adaptive Advantage Optimization (DVAO), which dynamically adjusts combination weights based on the empirical reward variance of each objective within a rollout group, effectively up-weighting objectives with a stronger learning signal while suppressing noisy ones. We mathematically prove that DVAO maintains bounded advantage magnitudes for stable training and introduces a self-adaptive cross-objective regularization mechanism. Extensive experiments on mathematical reasoning and tool-use benchmarks using Qwen3 and Qwen2.5 models demonstrate that DVAO significantly outperforms baseline methods, achieving a superior multi-objective Pareto frontier and robust training stability.

ru.title: Динамическая адаптация весов целей для стабильного обучения многоцелевых LLM
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод Dynamic Variance-adaptive Advantage Optimization (DVAO) для решения проблемы нестабильности обучения в многоцелевом обучении с подкреплением. Ключевая идея заключается в адаптивном взвешивании объективов на основе эмпирической дисперсии вознаграждений в пакете, что позволяет усилить сигналы обучения от информативных целей и подавить шумные. Авторы доказывают, что DVAO поддерживает ограниченные величины преимуществ (advantages) для стабильного обучения и вводит механизм само-адаптивной регуляризации между целями. Экспериментальная оценка на задачах математических рассуждений и использования инструментов показывает превосходство DVAO над базовыми методами с достижением лучшей Парето-границы.

en.title: Stabilizing Multi-Reward Learning with DVAO
en.desc: Dynamic Variance-adaptive Advantage Optimization (DVAO) is a novel approach designed to enhance training stability in multi-reward reinforcement learning scenarios. It adaptively adjusts the weighting of different objectives based on their empirical reward variance, allowing the model to focus on more informative signals while minimizing the impact of noisy rewards. This method overcomes the limitations of traditional scalarization techniques, which can lead to instability due to large advantage magnitudes or static hyperparameters. Through rigorous mathematical proof and extensive experiments, DVAO demonstrates improved performance and stability in multi-objective tasks compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 动态优化，稳定训练！
zh.desc: 动态方差自适应优势优化（DVAO）旨在解决多奖励强化学习中的训练不稳定性问题。它通过根据经验奖励方差自适应地加权目标，保持优势幅度的界限，从而提高多目标性能。与传统的奖励组合和优势组合方法相比，DVAO能够动态调整组合权重，有效抑制噪声目标，增强学习信号。实验结果表明，DVAO在数学推理和工具使用基准测试中显著优于基线方法，展现出更好的多目标帕累托前沿和稳定的训练效果。

papers.title: WBench: A Comprehensive Multi-turn Benchmark for Interactive Video World Model Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract WBench presents a comprehensive multi-turn benchmark for evaluating interactive world models across five dimensions using 289 test cases and 1,058 interaction turns with diverse scenarios and interaction types.  AI-generated summary Interactive world models are advancing rapidly, yet existing benchmarks cover only part of the required competencies, leaving no unified standard for systematic evaluation. To fill this gap, we introduce WBench, a comprehensive multi-turn benchmark for interactive world model evaluation along five dimensions, namely video quality, setting adherence, interaction adherence, consistency, and physics compliance. WBench contains 289 test cases and 1,058 interaction turns, where each case specifies a world setting and a multi-turn interaction sequence, covering diverse scenes, styles, subjects, and both first- and third-person perspectives, together with four interaction types, including navigation, subject action, event editing, and perspective switching. For navigation, WBench unifies text, 6-DoF pose, and discrete-action control, enabling evaluation of models with different native input interfaces. Evaluation uses 22 automatic sub-metrics that combine specialist vision models with large multimodal models, and all metrics are validated against human judgments. Across 20 state-of-the-art models, we find that no single model performs strongly across all dimensions. We provide detailed diagnostic insights into the characteristic strengths, weaknesses, and open challenges of each model. Code and data are available at https://github.com/meituan-longcat/WBench.

ru.title: Единый стандарт для оценки интерактивных мировых моделей
ru.desc: WBench представляет комплексный бенчмарк для оценки интерактивных мировых моделей, содержащий 289 тестовых случаев и 1,058 взаимодействий. Оценка проводится по пяти измерениям: качество видео, соответствие сценарию, соответствие взаимодействиям, консистентность и физическая правдоподобность. Бенчмарк включает четыре типа взаимодействий (навигация, действия субъекта, редактирование событий, переключение перспективы) и поддерживает разные интерфейсы управления. Автоматическая оценка использует 22 подметрики, основанные на специализированных зрительных моделях и больших мультимодальных моделях, валидированные относительно человеческих суждений.

en.title: WBench: A Unified Benchmark for Evaluating Interactive World Models
en.desc: WBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate interactive world models in a systematic way. It assesses models across five key dimensions: video quality, setting adherence, interaction adherence, consistency, and physics compliance. The benchmark includes 289 test cases and 1,058 interaction turns, covering a wide range of scenarios and interaction types, such as navigation and event editing. By using 22 automatic sub-metrics validated against human judgments, WBench reveals the strengths and weaknesses of various state-of-the-art models, highlighting the need for improvements in specific areas.

zh.title: WBench：全面评估交互式世界模型的基准测试
zh.desc: WBench是一个全面的多轮基准测试，用于评估交互式世界模型，涵盖视频质量、场景遵循、交互遵循、一致性和物理合规性五个维度。该基准包含289个测试案例和1058个交互回合，涉及多种场景、风格和视角，支持导航、主体动作、事件编辑和视角切换等四种交互类型。WBench通过统一文本、6自由度姿态和离散动作控制，评估不同输入接口的模型，并使用22个自动子指标进行评估，这些指标结合了专业视觉模型和大型多模态模型。研究发现，没有单一模型在所有维度上表现优异，提供了各模型的优缺点和挑战的详细诊断见解。

papers.title: Macaron-A2UI: A Model for Generative UI in Personal Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Generative UI models enable personal agents to synthesize dynamic interfaces with lightweight executable actions for enhanced interaction beyond text-only formats.  AI-generated summary As personal agents evolve to handle complex, user-centric tasks, static plain-text chat is rapidly becoming a bottleneck. Generative UI emerges as the necessary new interface layer, dynamically synthesizing the right controls, options, and state from the interaction context in real time. We present Macaron-A2UI, a model for Generative UI in personal agents. Our goal is to move beyond text-only interaction by enabling agents to generate natural language together with lightweight, executable UI actions for information collection, preference refinement, confirmation, and multi-goal organization. We build a large-scale Generative UI corpus from heterogeneous dialogue sources, introduce A2UI-Bench for controlled evaluation, and train 30B, 235B and 754B models with parameter-efficient LoRA-based supervised fine-tuning followed by reward-driven reinforcement learning. The best Macaron-A2UI model reaches 75.6 overall on A2UI-Bench without explicit schema hints, surpassing the strongest full-schema frontier baseline. We release the models, benchmark, and evaluation protocol to support future work on Generative UI for personal agents.

ru.title: От текста к интерфейсам: генеративные модели UI для умных агентов
ru.desc: Статья представляет Macaron-A2UI — модель для генерации пользовательских интерфейсов в персональных агентах, которая позволяет выходить за пределы текстового взаимодействия. Авторы создали большой корпус данных для обучения на основе диалоговых источников и разработали бенчмарк A2UI-Bench для оценки качества. Модели размером от 30B до 754B параметров были обучены с использованием параметрически-эффективной адаптации LoRA и reinforcement learning с функцией вознаграждения. Лучшая модель достигла 75.6% точности на бенчмарке без явных подсказок схемы, превзойдя существующие методы.

en.title: Empowering Personal Agents with Dynamic Generative UIs
en.desc: This paper introduces Macaron-A2UI, a model designed to enhance personal agents by enabling them to create dynamic user interfaces (UIs) that go beyond simple text interactions. The model synthesizes executable UI actions in real time, allowing for more engaging and efficient user interactions. By building a large-scale Generative UI corpus and developing the A2UI-Bench for evaluation, the authors demonstrate the model's effectiveness in generating natural language alongside UI elements. The results show that Macaron-A2UI outperforms existing models, paving the way for more advanced personal agent capabilities.

zh.title: 超越文本的动态交互：生成用户界面模型
zh.desc: 生成用户界面（Generative UI）模型使个人代理能够合成动态界面，并执行轻量级的可操作动作，从而增强交互体验。随着个人代理处理复杂用户任务的能力提升，静态文本聊天逐渐成为瓶颈。我们提出了Macaron-A2UI模型，旨在超越仅文本的交互，允许代理生成自然语言和轻量级的可执行用户界面动作。通过构建大规模的生成用户界面语料库和引入A2UI-Bench进行评估，我们的模型在没有明确模式提示的情况下，达到了75.6的整体评分，超越了最强的全模式基线。

papers.title: Foundation Protocol: A Coordination Layer for Agentic Society
papers.abstract: Abstract  Autonomous agents are moving from tools into a layer of social infrastructure: they browse, purchase, deploy software, manage systems, and increasingly interact with one another. As these systems scale, the bottleneck shifts away from raw model capability toward coordination. Agents need to form reliable relationships, organize multi-agent work, exchange value, support an AI economy, and stay safe and accountable under real-world oversight. This paper introduces the Foundation Protocol (FP), a graph-first coordination layer for an emerging human-AI society. FP unifies heterogeneous entities, including agents, tools, resources, humans, institutions, and organizations, and supports native multi-party organization and event-based collaboration. It also provides economic primitives for metering, receipts, and settlement, and treats policy, provenance, and audit as first-class concerns. FP is designed to wrap and bridge existing protocols rather than replace them, enabling incremental adoption while reducing integration and governance overhead. The aim is to keep autonomous agency composable while keeping accountability non-negotiable, so that coordination itself can become shared infrastructure for a human-AI society that is open, pluralistic, and governable.
affiliations: Singapore University of Technology and Design

ru.title: Протокол координации для общества людей и искусственных агентов
ru.desc: В статье представлен Foundation Protocol — граф-ориентированный протокол координации для взаимодействия автономных агентов в обществе. Протокол объединяет гетерогенные сущности (агентов, инструменты, ресурсы, людей и организации) и поддерживает многосторонний контроль событий с экономическими примитивами для учёта и расчётов. Foundation Protocol интегрируется с существующими протоколами, обеспечивая постепенное внедрение и снижение затрат на управление системой. Основная цель — сохранить композируемость автономных агентов, не снижая требования к подотчётности и управлению в человеко-AI обществе.

en.title: Empowering Autonomous Agents through Coordinated Collaboration
en.desc: This paper discusses the transition of autonomous agents from simple tools to essential components of social infrastructure. It highlights the importance of coordination among these agents as they scale, focusing on their ability to form relationships and collaborate effectively. The Foundation Protocol (FP) is introduced as a solution that facilitates multi-agent organization and collaboration while ensuring accountability and governance. By integrating existing protocols, FP aims to create a flexible and inclusive framework for a future where humans and AI can work together harmoniously.

zh.title: 基础协议：构建人类与AI的协作社会
zh.desc: 这篇论文讨论了自主代理如何从工具转变为社会基础设施的一部分。随着系统的扩展，协调成为了关键问题，代理需要建立可靠的关系，组织多代理工作，并在现实监督下保持安全和问责。论文介绍了基础协议（FP），这是一个以图为基础的协调层，旨在支持人类与人工智能的社会。FP统一了不同的实体，支持多方组织和事件驱动的协作，同时关注政策、来源和审计等重要问题。

papers.title: ParaVT: Taming the Tool Prior Paradox for Parallel Tool Use in Agentic Video Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract ParaVT enables parallel video tool calling through multi-agent reinforcement learning, addressing limitations of sequential approaches and improving long-video understanding performance.  AI-generated summary Training large multimodal models (LMMs) via reinforcement learning (RL) to natively invoke video-processing tools (e.g., cropping) has become a promising route to long-video understanding. However, existing native-RL methods dispatch tool calls sequentially (i.e., one per turn): a single wrong crop propagates errors without peer correction, multi-turn tool calls corrupt context, and inference cost scales linearly with the number of turns. We introduce ParaVT, the first multi-agent end-to-end RL-trained framework for Parallel Video Tool calling, dispatching multiple time-window crops in a single turn for cleaner context and better fault tolerance. Yet applying standard RL to ParaVT reveals an obstacle we term the Tool Prior Paradox: the pretrained tool priors that enable tool exploration also destabilize cold-started structural format and expose the skip-tool reward shortcut under temperature sampling. A cross-model contrast on a weaker-prior LMM supports this claim: format stays stable but RL elicits zero tool calls, indicating that prior strength is the shared driver of both format collapse and tool exploration. We propose PARA-GRPO (Parseability-Anchored and Ratio-gAted GRPO), which augments standard RL with two complementary mechanisms: (i) a targeted format reward applied only at the structural-token positions most prone to collapse, and (ii) a per-prompt frame-budget randomization that creates training prompts where calling the tool yields a measurable reward signal over skipping it. Across six long-video understanding benchmarks, ParaVT improves over the Qwen3-VL baseline by +7.9% on average, with PARA-GRPO lifting training-time format compliance from 0.13 to 0.64. As tool capabilities become increasingly internalized in modern LMMs, RL must cooperate with the resulting priors, and ParaVT offers a general recipe for agentic RL. Code, data, and model weights are publicly available.

ru.title: Параллельный вызов инструментов видеообработки через обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: Статья представляет ParaVT — фреймворк для параллельного вызова инструментов обработки видео с использованием многоагентного обучения с подкреплением. Авторы выявили проблему Tool Prior Paradox, когда предварительно обученные приоры инструментов одновременно улучшают исследование пространства действий и дестабилизируют формат вывода модели. Для решения этой проблемы предложен PARA-GRPO — алгоритм, который применяет таргетированное вознаграждение за правильный формат и рандомизирует бюджет кадров для создания стимула к вызову инструментов. Метод продемонстрировал улучшение качества понимания длинных видео на 7.9% в среднем по сравнению с базовыми моделями.

en.title: Revolutionizing Long-Video Understanding with Parallel Tool Calling
en.desc: ParaVT is a novel framework that enhances long-video understanding by enabling parallel video tool calling through multi-agent reinforcement learning (RL). Unlike traditional methods that process tool calls sequentially, ParaVT allows multiple tool calls in a single turn, improving context accuracy and reducing error propagation. The paper addresses challenges like the Tool Prior Paradox, where pretrained tool priors can destabilize the model's performance. By introducing PARA-GRPO, which combines targeted rewards and randomization, ParaVT significantly boosts performance on long-video benchmarks, demonstrating a new approach to integrating RL with multimodal models.

zh.title: 并行视频工具调用的创新之路
zh.desc: ParaVT是一个通过多智能体强化学习实现并行视频工具调用的框架，克服了传统顺序方法的局限性，提升了长视频理解的性能。该方法允许在单次调用中同时处理多个时间窗口的裁剪，提供更清晰的上下文和更好的容错能力。研究中提出的PARA-GRPO机制，通过针对性奖励和随机化训练提示，解决了工具调用中的格式不稳定问题。实验结果显示，ParaVT在六个长视频理解基准上平均提高了7.9%的性能，展示了其在现代多模态模型中的应用潜力。

papers.title: TriSplat: Simulation-Ready Feed-Forward 3D Scene Reconstruction
papers.abstract: Abstract TriSplat is a feed-forward 3D reconstruction network that uses oriented triangle primitives to directly generate simulation-ready meshes from single images, bypassing expensive post-processing steps.  AI-generated summary Sparse-view 3D reconstruction is increasingly addressed with feed-forward splatting networks that predict explicit primitives directly from images. Yet most existing methods remain centered on Gaussian primitives and expose surfaces only indirectly: extracting a usable mesh for downstream simulation, physics reasoning, or embodied interaction still requires expensive post-hoc steps that break the feed-forward promise. This limitation is especially pronounced in pose-free settings, where scene structure and camera parameters must be estimated jointly from sparse observations. We present TriSplat, a feed-forward reconstruction network that represents scenes with oriented triangle primitives and directly exports simulation-ready mesh scenes from a single forward pass. Given input images, the network predicts local 3D point maps, triangle attributes, camera poses, and optional intrinsics. Rather than regressing triangle orientation as an unconstrained latent variable, our approach constructs geometry normals from the predicted point maps, refines them with an image-conditioned normal head, and converts them into stable local frames for triangle parameterization. A mono-normal bootstrap schedule further stabilizes early training, while opacity and blur scheduling progressively sharpens the learned surface representation for direct mesh extraction. Experiments on RealEstate10K and DL3DV show that this representation produces more geometry-faithful reconstructions than Gaussian feed-forward baselines while maintaining competitive novel-view rendering quality. Because the rendering primitives are themselves surface triangles, the output can be directly ingested by physics engines, collision detectors, and standard rendering pipelines without any conversion, making it a practical simulation-ready solution for feed-forward 3D scene reconstruction.

ru.title: От изображения к симуляции: треугольники вместо гауссов
ru.desc: TriSplat — это нейросетевая архитектура для трёхмерной реконструкции сцен из одного изображения, которая использует ориентированные треугольные примитивы вместо традиционных гауссовых сплаттингов. Сеть предсказывает карты трёхмерных точек, атрибуты треугольников, позы камер и внутренние параметры в одном проходе, минуя дорогостоящие этапы постобработки. Авторы предлагают стабильный способ определения ориентации треугольников через геометрические нормали, полученные из предсказанных точечных карт и уточнённые специальной сетью. Результирующие меши напрямую совместимы с движками физики и стандартными графическими конвейерами, обеспечивая готовое к использованию решение для быстрой трёхмерной реконструкции.

en.title: Direct 3D Reconstruction with Oriented Triangle Primitives
en.desc: TriSplat is a novel feed-forward network designed for 3D reconstruction that utilizes oriented triangle primitives to create meshes directly from single images. Unlike traditional methods that rely on Gaussian primitives and require additional processing to extract usable meshes, TriSplat generates simulation-ready outputs in a single pass. The network predicts local 3D point maps, triangle attributes, and camera parameters, allowing for a more efficient reconstruction process. By employing techniques like mono-normal bootstrap and opacity scheduling, TriSplat enhances the stability and quality of the generated surfaces, making it suitable for real-time applications in physics and rendering.

zh.title: TriSplat：直接生成仿真准备网格的3D重建新方法
zh.desc: TriSplat是一种前馈式3D重建网络，利用定向三角形原语直接从单张图像生成可用于仿真的网格，省去了昂贵的后处理步骤。该方法通过预测局部3D点图、三角形属性和相机姿态，能够在一次前向传递中直接导出仿真准备好的网格场景。与传统的高斯原语方法相比，TriSplat在几何重建上更为精确，并且可以直接与物理引擎和渲染管道兼容使用。实验结果表明，TriSplat在保持竞争性新视图渲染质量的同时，提供了更忠实于几何形状的重建。

papers.title: QUEST: Training Frontier Deep Research Agents with Fully Synthetic Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract QUEST is an open-family of deep research agents trained with synthesized data and reinforcement learning to perform well across diverse long-horizon search tasks.  AI-generated summary Deep research agents extend the role of search engines from retrieving keyword-matched pages to synthesizing knowledge, fundamentally changing how humans interact with information. However, frontier systems remain proprietary, while existing open agents often generalize poorly across different task types, leaving unclear how to train a broadly capable deep research agent. We release QUEST, a family of open models (ranging from 2B to 35B) that serve as general-purpose deep research agents designed to handle a wide range of long-horizon search tasks, with strong capabilities in fact seeking, citation grounding, and report synthesis. To build QUEST, we propose an effective training recipe combining mid-training, supervised fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning. Central to this recipe is a curated data synthesis pipeline based on unified rubric trees, which applies to different task types and enables synthesizing training data with verifiable rewards without human annotation. In addition, QUEST incorporates a built-in context management mechanism that enables effective long-horizon reasoning and knowledge synthesis. Using only 8K synthesized tasks, QUEST approaches or even surpasses frontier closed-source agents across eight deep research benchmarks spanning diverse task types, and achieves the best overall performance among recent open-weight agents. We released everything: models, data, and training scripts.

ru.title: Открытые агенты глубокого поиска через синтез данных и подкрепленное обучение
ru.desc: QUEST — это семейство открытых глубоких агентов поиска, обученных на синтезированных данных с помощью обучения с подкреплением для выполнения разнообразных долгосрочных задач поиска. Авторы предлагают эффективный рецепт обучения, сочетающий mid-training, supervised fine-tuning и reinforcement learning, с использованием конвейера синтеза данных на основе унифицированных деревьев рубрик. Встроенный механизм управления контекстом позволяет модели эффективно проводить долгосрочные рассуждения и синтез знаний. На основе всего 8000 синтезированных задач QUEST достигает или превосходит закрытые коммерческие системы по восьми бенчмаркам глубокого поиска.

en.title: Revolutionizing Search with Open Deep Research Agents
en.desc: QUEST is a new family of deep research agents that use reinforcement learning and synthesized data to excel in various long-horizon search tasks. Unlike traditional search engines that only retrieve information, QUEST synthesizes knowledge, enhancing how users interact with data. The training process involves a unique combination of mid-training, supervised fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning, supported by a data synthesis pipeline that generates training data with measurable rewards. With models ranging from 2B to 35B parameters, QUEST demonstrates strong performance in fact-seeking, citation grounding, and report synthesis, outperforming many proprietary systems with just 8,000 synthesized tasks.

zh.title: QUEST：开放式深度研究代理的未来
zh.desc: QUEST是一个开放的深度研究代理家族，使用合成数据和强化学习进行训练，旨在处理多样化的长时间搜索任务。它的目标是超越传统搜索引擎的功能，不仅仅是检索关键词匹配的页面，而是合成知识，改变人类与信息的互动方式。通过结合中期训练、监督微调和强化学习的有效训练方法，QUEST能够在不同任务类型中表现出色。我们发布了所有模型、数据和训练脚本，推动了开放式深度研究代理的发展。

papers.title: Toward Native Multimodal Modeling: A Roadmap
papers.abstract: Abstract Native multimodal modeling advances beyond traditional fusion approaches by integrating modalities inherently within a unified transformer framework, enabling seamless understanding and generation across diverse input-output configurations.  AI-generated summary Multimodal modeling represents a vital step from modality-agnostic reasoning toward world modeling. While early approaches predominantly rely on late-fusion that assembles encoders and frozen language backbones with output heads, recent efforts have shifted the paradigm toward native multimodal modeling (NMM) with the intrinsic integration of modalities for superior multimodal performance. Despite its potential, the design space of native architectures remains insufficiently defined. In this paper, we present the community with a formalized roadmap for this transition. Specifically, we formally define the architectural nativity, distinguishing mid-fusion and early-fusion from non-native paradigms. We further organize the existing native models through the lens of input-output duality into three categories: (i) Multi-to-Text for cross-modal comprehension with text-only output; (ii) Multi-to-Target for scenario-oriented generation, e.g., image, audio and video generation, and (iii) Multi-to-Multi for unified modeling with symmetric input-output. We deliver a comprehensive and industrial-grade investigation into the transition toward the definitive NMM framework, where understanding and generation seamlessly coexist within a unified transformer paradigm. We systematically unpack the end-to-end pipeline from industrial perspectives from architectural coordination, massive data curation, to full-stack training recipes, inference & deployment, and the comprehensive evaluation for truly native modeling.

ru.title: Единая архитектура для понимания и генерации всех модальностей
ru.desc: В работе представляется формальная классификация архитектур native multimodal modeling (NMM), которая интегрирует различные модальности непосредственно внутри единого трансформера, а не через позднее слияние отдельных энкодеров. Авторы предлагают дорожную карту для перехода от модальностно-агностичного рассуждения к моделированию мира, организуя существующие нативные модели по схеме входного-выходного дуализма на три категории: Multi-to-Text, Multi-to-Target и Multi-to-Multi. В статье проводится комплексное исследование полного pipeline разработки NMM-систем, охватывающее архитектурную координацию, подготовку данных, тренировку, инференс и оценку. Работа устанавливает четкие критерии различия между нативным моделированием и ненативными парадигмами, обеспечивая промышленный стандарт для разработки многомодальных систем.

en.title: Seamless Integration: The Future of Multimodal Modeling
en.desc: This paper discusses native multimodal modeling (NMM), which integrates different types of data (like text, images, and audio) within a single transformer framework. Unlike traditional methods that combine outputs after processing each modality separately, NMM allows for a more cohesive understanding and generation of information. The authors propose a structured approach to define and categorize native architectures, distinguishing between different types of data integration methods. They also provide insights into the practical aspects of implementing NMM, including data management and evaluation techniques.

zh.title: 原生多模态建模的未来之路
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了原生多模态建模（NMM），超越了传统的融合方法，通过在统一的变换器框架中内在整合不同模态，实现了对多样输入输出配置的无缝理解和生成。我们定义了原生架构的特性，并将现有的原生模型根据输入输出的双重性分为三类：多对文本、多对目标和多对多。论文还提供了从工业角度出发的全面调查，涵盖了架构协调、大规模数据整理、全栈训练方案、推理与部署，以及对真正原生建模的综合评估。通过这些研究，我们为多模态建模的未来发展提供了清晰的路线图。

papers.title: AutoResearch AI: Towards AI-Powered Research Automation for Scientific Discovery
papers.abstract: Abstract AI systems are evolving from task-specific assistants to workflow-level research automators, facing challenges in autonomy, reproducibility, and accountability across scientific domains.  AI-generated summary Scientific research is being reshaped by AI systems that move beyond isolated assistance toward longer-horizon workflows spanning literature grounding, hypothesis generation, experimentation, validation, reporting, and revision. This shift marks a transition from task-level AI for science to workflow-level research automation. Yet current systems remain fragmented, differing in autonomy, domain scope, execution environment, validation mechanism, and human oversight, while still struggling with evidence preservation, reproducibility, weak-direction rejection, provenance tracking, cross-domain robustness, and accountable scientific closure. This survey examines these developments through AutoResearch, defined as the developmental spectrum of AI-powered scientific workflow automation. Within it, Vibe Research denotes the human-steered region of prompt-based assistance and human-verified execution, whereas emerging AI-led systems coordinate larger portions of the discovery loop without achieving robust autonomy. We analyze how research systems redistribute control, evidence, execution, validation, and accountability across workflows and organize the field around five workflow conditions: literature and research grounding; hypothesis formation and planning; experimentation and tool use; feedback, validation, and review; and reporting and knowledge communication. We further synthesize AI scientist systems, mixed-initiative co-research frameworks, benchmarks, domain deployments, and open-source infrastructures. Finally, we propose five evaluation dimensions--novelty, validity, impact, reliability, and provenance--and show that AutoResearch autonomy is domain-conditioned, being more credible in structured, executable, and rapidly verifiable settings but limited in embodied, delayed, heterogeneous, ethical, or institutionally accountable contexts.
affiliations: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: От помощника к автоматизатору: как AI трансформирует научные исследования
ru.desc: В статье рассматривается эволюция AI-систем от специализированных помощников к автоматизаторам научных рабочих потоков, которые охватывают поиск литературы, генерацию гипотез, экспериментирование, валидацию и составление отчётов. Авторы вводят концепцию AutoResearch для описания спектра разработки AI-powered автоматизации научных процессов, выделяя человеко-управляемые системы подсказок и системы, управляемые AI. В работе анализируются пять ключевых этапов научного рабочего потока и различные компоненты систем, включая распределение контроля, сохранение доказательств, воспроизводимость и подотчётность. Исследование предлагает пять критериев оценки автономности AI-систем в науке, показывая, что их надёжность зависит от структурированности и проверяемости задач, но ограничена в областях с этическими и институциональными требованиями.

en.title: From Task-Specific AI to Workflow-Level Research Automation
en.desc: This paper discusses the evolution of AI systems from simple task-specific tools to more complex workflow-level research automators in scientific research. It highlights the challenges these systems face, such as ensuring reproducibility, accountability, and effective human oversight. The authors introduce the concept of AutoResearch, which encompasses the automation of scientific workflows, and Vibe Research, which focuses on human-guided AI assistance. They propose evaluation criteria for these systems and emphasize that the effectiveness of AI in research varies significantly across different domains and contexts.

zh.title: AI驱动的科学研究自动化新纪元
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了人工智能系统在科学研究中的演变，从单一任务助手转向工作流级别的研究自动化。AI系统面临自主性、可重复性和问责制等挑战，尤其是在文献基础、假设生成、实验验证等方面。论文定义了AutoResearch，强调了AI驱动的科学工作流自动化的发展，并分析了人类与AI的协作模式。最后，提出了五个评估维度，以评估AI在不同领域的自主性和可靠性。

papers.title: Your Embedding Model is SMARTer Than You Think
papers.abstract: Abstract SMART enhances multimodal retrieval by leveraging latent multi-vector capabilities from single-vector models through contrastive training and late-interaction inference, achieving state-of-the-art performance with reduced computational costs.  AI-generated summary Multimodal retrieval relies heavily on single-vector retrievers, which compress rich, sequential token sequences into one single global representation. While efficient, they discard fine-grained, local evidence critical for dense retrieval tasks. Multi-vector approaches were introduced as a solution, but they strictly require training and many ignore the necessity of a globally summarizing representation. To address this, we introduce SMART, a framework that unlocks the latent multi-vector capabilities of standard single-vector models. We first demonstrate that standard contrastive training on the pooled embedding implicitly shapes the retrieval geometry of preceding hidden states via gradient flow. By applying direct late-interaction over these frozen hidden states during inference, SMART acts as a plug-and-play upgrade that consistently improves performance across diverse modalities, improving even the state-of-the-art models further on MMEB-V2. We also reveal SMART's superior performance, as simple lightweight post-training not only saves time and compute, but also brings forth further improvement on Visual Document retrieval, allowing a single-vector model to outperform SoTA multi-vector counterparts. Ultimately, SMART offers both a highly efficient inference enhancement and a powerful finetuning technique for multimodal retrieval. We open source our code and weights at https://github.com/HanSolo9682/SMART.

ru.title: Раскрытие мультивекторного потенциала через контрастивное обучение и ленивое взаимодействие
ru.desc: В статье представляется SMART — фреймворк, который раскрывает скрытые возможности мультивекторного поиска в стандартных однвекторных моделях для мультимодального retrieval. Авторы показывают, что контрастивное обучение на объединённых эмбеддингах неявно формирует геометрию поиска в скрытых слоях модели через градиентный поток. Применяя late-interaction поиск над замороженными скрытыми состояниями во время инференса, SMART работает как модульное улучшение, повышающее производительность на различных модальностях. Метод одновременно уменьшает вычислительные затраты и позволяет однвекторным моделям превзойти современные мультивекторные подходы.

en.title: Unlocking Multi-Vector Power in Single-Vector Models
en.desc: SMART is a framework designed to improve multimodal retrieval by enhancing single-vector models with multi-vector capabilities through contrastive training. It effectively retains important local information that is often lost in traditional single-vector approaches, which summarize data into a single representation. By utilizing late-interaction inference, SMART allows for better performance without the need for extensive retraining, making it a cost-effective solution. This method not only boosts retrieval accuracy across various modalities but also enables single-vector models to surpass state-of-the-art multi-vector models.

zh.title: SMART：高效的多模态检索升级方案
zh.desc: SMART是一种增强多模态检索的框架，它通过对比训练和后期交互推理，利用单向量模型的潜在多向量能力，从而实现了最先进的性能并降低了计算成本。传统的单向量检索器将丰富的序列信息压缩为单一的全局表示，虽然高效，但会丢失对密集检索任务至关重要的细粒度局部证据。SMART通过对标准单向量模型的潜在多向量能力进行解锁，提供了一种即插即用的升级方案，能够在不同模态上持续提升性能。最终，SMART不仅提高了推理效率，还为多模态检索提供了强大的微调技术。

papers.title: Pantheon360: Taming Digital Twin Generation via 3D-Aware 360° Video Diffusion
papers.abstract: Abstract Pantheon360 enables high-fidelity 360° video generation for digital twins by combining 3D-aware diffusion with explicit geometric caching to ensure spatial-temporal consistency.  AI-generated summary Generating complete digital twins from videos requires precise camera control, global scene coverage, and strict spatial-temporal consistency constraints that remain challenging for perspective video generators due to their limited field of view (FoV). Their narrow FoV forces long or multi-view trajectories, amplifying cross-view inconsistency and temporal drift. We argue that 360° video generation offers a natural solution: panoramic coverage simplifies trajectory design and provides a strong global context for maintaining coherence. We introduce Pantheon360: Taming Digital Twin Generation via 3D-Aware 360° Video Diffusion, a controllable 360° video generation framework that synthesizes high-fidelity videos from sparse 360° inputs. The key idea is an explicit 3D Cache, reconstructed from the input, which serves as a geometric scaffold for any user-defined camera path. This allows the diffusion model to focus on photorealistic texture refinement while the 3D Cache enforces global geometric consistency. Experiments show that Pantheon360 achieves superior visual quality and unmatched geometric coherence, enabling reliable and flexible 360° scene generation for downstream simulation and digital-twin applications.
affiliations: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University

ru.title: 360° видео без шва: геометрия и диффузия вместе
ru.desc: Pantheon360 — это новая система для создания высокачественного 360° видео цифровых двойников, которая объединяет 3D-осведомлённую диффузионную модель с явным геометрическим кэшированием. Система решает проблему пространственно-временной согласованности, которая возникает при генерации видео с ограниченным полем зрения, используя панорамное покрытие сцены. Ключевая инновация — явный 3D-кэш, реконструированный из входных данных, который служит геометрической подложкой для любого пути камеры. Диффузионная модель может сосредоточиться на фотореалистичном уточнении текстуры, в то время как 3D-кэш обеспечивает глобальную геометрическую согласованность.

en.title: Revolutionizing Digital Twin Creation with 360° Video Generation
en.desc: Pantheon360 is a framework designed for generating high-quality 360° videos that can be used to create digital twins. It combines 3D-aware diffusion techniques with a geometric caching system to maintain consistency in both space and time. This approach addresses the limitations of traditional perspective video generators, which struggle with narrow fields of view and can lead to inconsistencies in the generated content. By using a 3D Cache, Pantheon360 allows for better camera control and enhances the visual quality of the generated videos, making it ideal for applications in simulation and digital twins.

zh.title: Pantheon360：高保真 360° 视频生成的创新之路
zh.desc: Pantheon360 是一种高保真 360° 视频生成框架，旨在为数字双胞胎创建提供支持。它结合了 3D 感知扩散和显式几何缓存，以确保空间和时间的一致性。通过使用 360° 视频，Pantheon360 简化了轨迹设计，并提供了强大的全局上下文，从而提高了生成视频的质量。实验结果表明，Pantheon360 在视觉质量和几何一致性方面表现优越，适用于下游模拟和数字双胞胎应用。

papers.title: Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User's Digital World
papers.abstract: Abstract Claw-Anything benchmark evaluates large language model agents on comprehensive user activity contexts spanning extended timeframes, multiple services, and diverse device interactions to assess true always-on personal assistance capabilities.  AI-generated summary Large language model agents are increasingly envisioned as always-on personal assistants with access to anything relevant in the user's digital world. Yet current systems operate over only narrow slices of that world, limiting context-sensitive reasoning and effective assistance. Existing benchmarks similarly provide only partial user state and therefore fail to capture performance in such a broad, always-on setting. To address this gap, we introduce Claw-Anything, a benchmark that expands agent context along three dimensions: long-horizon activity histories, interdependent backend services, and integrated GUI and CLI interaction across multiple devices. To instantiate this setting, we simulate months of user activity through multi-round event injection, producing complex world states and realistic noise, including irrelevant events and conflicting signals. Agents must reason over rich contextual environments while remaining robust to such noise. This expanded scope also enables the evaluation of proactive assistance, requiring agents to anticipate user needs and deliver timely recommendations. Experiments show that GPT-5.5 achieves only 34.5% pass@1, substantially below prior benchmarks, underscoring a gap between current agent capabilities and the demands of always-on personal assistance. Alongside the benchmark, we release an automated data-generation pipeline that yields 2,000 training environments and improves the base model by 23.7%, demonstrating its utility of scalable data infrastructure.
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: От узких срезов к полной картине: бенчмарк для действительно вездесущего ассистента
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк Claw-Anything для оценки способности больших языковых моделей (LLM) выступать в роли постоянно доступных персональных помощников с доступом к полному контексту цифровой жизни пользователя. Бенчмарк расширяет оценку вдоль трёх измерений: долгосрочная история активности, взаимозависимые бэкенд-сервисы и интегрированное взаимодействие через графический и командный интерфейсы на нескольких устройствах. Авторы симулируют месячную активность пользователя с реалистичным шумом, включая посторонние события и противоречивые сигналы, требуя от агентов устойчивости и способности к проактивной помощи. Результаты показывают значительный разрыв между текущими возможностями LLM-агентов (GPT-4.5 достигает только 34.5% по метрике pass@1) и требованиями истинно постоянно работающего персонального ассистента.

en.title: Claw-Anything: Redefining Always-On Personal Assistance
en.desc: The Claw-Anything benchmark evaluates large language model agents in a realistic setting that mimics continuous user interactions across various devices and services. It addresses the limitations of existing benchmarks by incorporating long-term activity histories and complex user states, allowing for better context-sensitive reasoning. The benchmark challenges agents to provide proactive assistance by anticipating user needs amidst noise and conflicting information. Results indicate that current models, like GPT-5.5, struggle to meet the demands of true always-on personal assistance, highlighting the need for improved capabilities in this area.

zh.title: 评估始终在线个人助手能力的新基准
zh.desc: Claw-Anything基准测试评估大型语言模型代理在广泛用户活动上下文中的表现，涵盖了长时间框架、多种服务和多样设备的交互，以评估真正的始终在线个人助手能力。现有系统仅在狭窄的环境中运行，限制了上下文敏感推理和有效的帮助。Claw-Anything通过扩展代理上下文，模拟用户活动的复杂世界状态，要求代理在丰富的上下文环境中进行推理，同时对噪声保持鲁棒性。实验表明，GPT-5.5的表现远低于预期，显示出当前代理能力与始终在线个人助手需求之间的差距。

papers.title: On-Policy Adversarial Flow Distillation for Autoregressive Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Adversarial Flow Distillation enables efficient distillation of heterogeneous video generation models by using on-policy feedback and forward-process flow-matching updates without requiring teacher scores or detailed trajectory information.  AI-generated summary Autoregressive video generators are attractive for streaming, long-horizon, and interactive applications, but distilling strong black-box teachers into causal students remains difficult. The student must learn under its own rollout distribution, whereas practical teachers may expose only prompt-conditioned completed videos and may differ in architecture, capacity, temporal design, and sampling schedule. This interface makes supervised fine-tuning off-policy, score-based distillation inapplicable, and direct adversarial imitation too sparse for denoising-time credit assignment. We propose Adversarial Flow Distillation (AFD), an on-policy framework for heterogeneous black-box video distillation. AFD queries the teacher and rolls out the current student on the same prompts, trains a prompt-paired Bradley-Terry discriminator to estimate clean-sample teacher-student discrepancy, and converts the resulting on-policy advantage into forward-process flow-matching updates on the student's own noised states. Thus, AFD provides dense velocity-field supervision while requiring no teacher scores, latents, denoising trajectories, step alignment, or reverse-chain reinforcement learning. Experiments across two causal AR student families show that AFD consistently improves motion- and physics-sensitive generation while preserving general video quality, and ablations validate the importance of adaptive on-policy feedback and forward-process credit assignment. The method requires only clean teacher videos and student rollouts, providing a practical route for distilling proprietary or heterogeneous video generators into efficient autoregressive students.

ru.title: Дистилляция разнородных видеогенераторов через адверзарное сопоставление потоков
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод Adversarial Flow Distillation для эффективной дистилляции моделей генерации видео, когда учитель и ученик имеют разную архитектуру. Подход использует on-policy обратную связь от дискриминатора, который оценивает различие между видео учителя и ученика на одних и тех же промптах. Полученные оценки преобразуются в обновления flow-matching модели в forward-процессе, обеспечивая плотное обучение без необходимости доступа к внутренним представлениям учителя. Метод показывает улучшение качества генерации видео, особенно для движения и физики, требуя только финальные видео от учителя и rollout'ы ученика.

en.title: Efficient Video Generation with Adversarial Flow Distillation
en.desc: Adversarial Flow Distillation (AFD) is a novel approach for efficiently training video generation models by leveraging on-policy feedback. It allows a student model to learn from a teacher model without needing detailed scores or trajectory data, which is often unavailable in practice. AFD uses a discriminator to assess the differences between the teacher's and student's outputs, enabling the student to improve its performance through flow-matching updates. This method enhances the student's ability to generate high-quality videos while maintaining the dynamics of motion and physics, making it suitable for various applications.

zh.title: 对抗流动蒸馏：高效视频生成的创新方法
zh.desc: 对抗流动蒸馏（AFD）是一种高效的异构视频生成模型蒸馏方法，它通过使用在线反馈和前向过程流匹配更新来实现，而无需教师评分或详细的轨迹信息。该方法解决了在自回归视频生成中，如何将强大的黑箱教师模型转化为因果学生模型的难题。AFD通过在相同提示下查询教师并展开当前学生，训练一个配对的Bradley-Terry判别器来估计教师与学生之间的差异，从而实现密集的速度场监督。实验表明，AFD在保持视频质量的同时，显著提高了运动和物理敏感的生成效果。

papers.title: ControlLight: Towards Controllable, Consistent, and Generalizable Low-Light Enhancement
papers.abstract: Abstract ControlLight is a controllable low-light enhancement framework that uses a large-scale real-world dataset and weighted flow matching loss to ensure consistent image quality across varying enhancement strengths.  AI-generated summary Existing deep learning-based low-light enhancement methods are typically trained on limited datasets with single enhancement targets, which restricts their generalization ability and controllability in real-world applications. To overcome these limitations, we propose ControlLight, a controllable, consistent, and generalizable framework for low-light enhancement. We first construct a large-scale dataset of real-world degraded images with continuous illumination-strength supervision. To further ensure consistent outputs under different control strengths, we introduce a misalignment-aware weighted flow matching loss that preserves image structure across continuous enhancement strengths. ControlLight allows users to edit real-world degraded low-light images toward satisfactory enhancement results by flexibly controlling the strength while preserving visual consistency and realism. Extensive experiments show that ControlLight achieves state-of-the-art performance against existing low-light enhancement approaches while demonstrating strong continuous controllability and generalization to real-world scenarios.
affiliations: Shenzhen Institutes of Advanced Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Управляемое улучшение фото в темноте с контролем качества
ru.desc: ControlLight — это фреймворк для управляемого улучшения изображений при низкой освещённости, который использует большой набор данных реальных деградированных изображений. Модель обучается с непрерывной супервизией по уровню освещённости, что позволяет гибко контролировать силу обработки. Авторы вводят специальную weighted flow matching loss функцию, которая сохраняет структуру изображения при изменении параметров улучшения. Метод показывает лучшие результаты среди существующих подходов и хорошо обобщается на реальные сценарии с разными условиями освещения.

en.title: Control Your Light: Enhance Low-Light Images with Precision!
en.desc: ControlLight is a framework designed to enhance low-light images while allowing users to control the level of enhancement. It addresses the limitations of previous methods that were trained on small datasets and focused on single enhancement targets, which hindered their effectiveness in real-world situations. By creating a large-scale dataset of real-world images and employing a unique weighted flow matching loss, ControlLight ensures that the quality of images remains consistent across different enhancement strengths. This approach not only improves the performance of low-light enhancement but also provides users with the flexibility to achieve desired results while maintaining the realism of the images.

zh.title: 可控的一致性低光增强框架
zh.desc: ControlLight 是一个可控的低光增强框架，利用大规模真实世界数据集和加权流匹配损失，确保在不同增强强度下图像质量的一致性。现有的深度学习低光增强方法通常在有限的数据集上训练，目标单一，限制了它们在实际应用中的泛化能力和可控性。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了 ControlLight，一个可控、一致且具有良好泛化能力的低光增强框架。通过构建一个包含连续照明强度监督的大规模真实退化图像数据集，ControlLight 允许用户灵活控制增强强度，同时保持视觉一致性和真实感。

papers.title: SkillEvolBench: Benchmarking the Evolution from Episodic Experience to Procedural Skills
papers.abstract: Abstract Current large language model agents struggle to form robust reusable skills from episodic experience, with raw trajectory reuse often outperforming distilled skills due to discarded contextual cues.  AI-generated summary Large language model (LLM) agents accumulate rich episodic trajectories while solving real-world tasks, but it remains unclear whether such experience can be distilled into reusable procedural skills. We introduce SkillEvolBench, a diagnostic benchmark for evaluating this step from experience reuse to skill formation. It contains 180 tasks across six real-world agent environments, organized into role-conditioned task families with shared latent procedures. Agents learn from acquisition tasks, update an external skill library using compacted trajectories and verifier feedback, and then face frozen deployment tasks testing context shift, adversarial shortcuts, and composition. By comparing self-generated and curated-start skill evolution against no-skill and raw-trajectory controls, SkillEvolBench separates procedural abstraction from base capability, curated prior knowledge, and direct reuse of episodic traces. Across ten model configurations and three agent harnesses, we find that current agents often adapt locally but rarely form robust reusable skills. Skill-based conditions can improve acquisition or replay, and individual models sometimes gain on specific deployment axes, but these gains are unstable under frozen deployment. Raw-trajectory reuse frequently outperforms distilled skills, suggesting that current abstraction procedures discard contextual and procedural cues that remain useful for future tasks. Capacity and cost analyses further show that writing more skills or larger Tier-3 resource libraries is not sufficient: additional updates can improve coverage while introducing episode-specific drift and procedural clutter. These findings position SkillEvolBench as a testbed for measuring when one-off experience becomes durable procedural knowledge rather than task-local memory.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: От эпизодического опыта к стабильным навыкам: когда память становится мастерством
ru.desc: Работа исследует проблему превращения опыта в переиспользуемые навыки для агентов на основе больших языковых моделей. Авторы предлагают SkillEvolBench — бенчмарк с 180 задачами для оценки того, как агенты учатся из траекторий и обновляют библиотеку навыков с помощью обратной связи. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что текущие модели часто не формируют устойчивые переиспользуемые процедуры, так как обучение навыкам отбрасывает важный контекст и процедурные сигналы. Исследование демонстрирует, что простое переиспользование исходных траекторий часто превосходит дистиллированные навыки, что указывает на необходимость более эффективных методов абстракции знаний.

en.title: From Experience to Skills: Bridging the Gap in AI Learning
en.desc: This paper discusses the challenges faced by large language model agents in transforming episodic experiences into reusable skills. The authors introduce SkillEvolBench, a benchmark designed to evaluate the transition from experience reuse to skill formation across various tasks. Their findings indicate that while agents can adapt to specific tasks, they struggle to develop robust, reusable skills, often relying on raw trajectory reuse instead of distilled skills. The study highlights the importance of contextual cues in skill formation and suggests that simply increasing the number of skills or resources is not enough to enhance performance.

zh.title: 从经验到技能的转变：SkillEvolBench的探索
zh.desc: 当前的大型语言模型代理在从经验中形成可重用的技能方面存在困难，原始轨迹的重用往往优于提炼的技能，因为后者丢弃了上下文线索。我们提出了SkillEvolBench，这是一个用于评估从经验重用到技能形成的诊断基准。该基准包含180个任务，分布在六个真实世界的代理环境中，任务按角色条件组织，具有共享的潜在程序。研究表明，当前的代理通常在局部适应，但很少形成稳健的可重用技能，原始轨迹重用经常优于提炼技能，表明现有的抽象过程丢弃了对未来任务仍然有用的上下文和程序线索。

papers.title: InstructSAM: Segment Any Instance with Any Instructions
papers.abstract: Abstract InstructSAM presents a unified framework for multi-instance segmentation using instruction-driven queries that bridge vision-language models and SAM3 through learnable instance queries and hybrid attention mechanisms.  AI-generated summary In this paper, we introduce InstructSAM, a unified and streamlined framework designed for multi-instance segmentation under arbitrary instructions. We formulates instruction-driven instance segmentation as a set-structured query prediction problem and propose an explicit reasoning-to-instance query interface that elegantly bridges a vision-language model (VLM) and SAM3. Specifically, a bank of learnable instance queries is injected into the VLM and contextualized with instruction and visual information, enabling each query to serve as an instance-aware slot. A hybrid-attention mechanism further promotes interaction among these queries, visual tokens, and instruction tokens, improving instance enumeration and reducing duplicate predictions. The resulting LLM-conditioned queries are projected into SAM3's detector query space to drive accurate multi-instance segmentation in a single forward pass. This design equips SAM3 with high-level instruction understanding, compositional reasoning, and instance-level set prediction without modifying its core architecture. To support training and evaluation, we further construct Inst2Seg, a high-quality and large-scale instruction-based instance segmentation dataset and benchmark that couples free-form instructions with instance-level masks. Extensive experiments show that only 2B-scale InstructSAM achieves strong results across complex instruction-driven and phrase-level referring segmentation benchmarks, outperforming prior end-to-end methods and SAM3's agentic pipeline while enabling efficient single-pass multi-instance prediction.
affiliations: Nanjing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics

ru.title: Инструкции вместо масок: объединение языка и зрения для точной сегментации объектов
ru.desc: InstructSAM представляет унифицированный фреймворк для сегментации множественных объектов, который соединяет модели зрения и языка с SAM3 через обучаемые запросы экземпляров. Система формулирует задачу сегментации как предсказание структурированного набора запросов, где каждый запрос действует как слот, осознающий конкретный объект. Гибридный механизм внимания улучшает взаимодействие между запросами, визуальными токенами и инструкциями, что снижает дублирование предсказаний. Авторы также создали новый датасет Inst2Seg с высококачественными аннотациями для обучения и оценки моделей сегментации по естественноязычным инструкциям.

en.title: InstructSAM: Bridging Vision and Language for Enhanced Segmentation
en.desc: InstructSAM is a new framework that enhances multi-instance segmentation by using instruction-driven queries. It combines vision-language models with a segmentation model called SAM3 through a set of learnable instance queries. These queries are designed to understand both visual and instruction information, allowing for better identification of instances without duplicating predictions. The framework also introduces a large dataset called Inst2Seg for training and evaluation, demonstrating superior performance in segmentation tasks compared to previous methods.

zh.title: 指令驱动的多实例分割新框架
zh.desc: InstructSAM 是一个统一的框架，用于基于指令的多实例分割。它将指令驱动的实例分割问题形式化为集合结构的查询预测问题，并通过可学习的实例查询和混合注意力机制将视觉语言模型与 SAM3 连接起来。该框架通过注入可学习的实例查询，结合指令和视觉信息，使每个查询能够作为实例感知的槽位。实验表明，InstructSAM 在复杂的指令驱动和短语级引用分割基准上表现优异，超越了之前的端到端方法。

papers.title: Geometry-Aware Image Flow Matching
papers.abstract: Abstract Geometry-aware generative models leveraging spherical manifolds and optimal transport techniques outperform traditional Euclidean approaches for natural image synthesis.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in generative models highlight the power of geometry-aware modeling in manifold-constrained settings. Yet, for natural images, the field remains confined to Euclidean assumptions, failing to exploit the potential of intrinsic geometric structures within the data. In this work, we investigate the geometry of natural images and observe that semantic information is predominantly encoded in directional components, while norm components can be approximated by the global average. This property holds across both RGB and latent spaces, suggesting that natural images can be effectively modeled on a hypersphere. Building on this finding, we introduce Spherical Optimal Transport Flow Matching (SOT-CFM), which utilizes angular distance, and Spherical Flow Matching (SFM), which constrains dynamics directly on the manifold. Our experiments demonstrate that these geometry-aware methods achieve superior performance against Euclidean baselines. Ultimately, this work provides a novel perspective that bridges the gap between Riemannian manifold-based modeling and natural image generation.

ru.title: Синтез изображений на сфере: оптимальный транспорт вместо евклидова пространства
ru.desc: В работе исследуется геометрическая структура естественных изображений и обнаруживается, что семантическая информация кодируется преимущественно в направленных компонентах, а нормовые компоненты можно аппроксимировать глобальным средним. На основе этого наблюдения авторы предлагают два новых метода: Spherical Optimal Transport Flow Matching (SOT-CFM), использующий угловое расстояние, и Spherical Flow Matching (SFM), ограничивающий динамику непосредственно на многообразии. Эти геометрически-ориентированные подходы демонстрируют превосходные результаты по сравнению с евклидовыми методами синтеза изображений. Работа устанавливает связь между моделированием на основе риманова многообразия и синтезом естественных изображений.

en.title: Harnessing Geometry for Superior Image Generation
en.desc: This paper explores the use of geometry-aware generative models for creating natural images, focusing on spherical manifolds instead of traditional Euclidean spaces. It highlights that important semantic information in images is primarily found in their directional components, while the overall intensity can be simplified. The authors introduce two new methods, Spherical Optimal Transport Flow Matching (SOT-CFM) and Spherical Flow Matching (SFM), which leverage angular distances and operate directly on the manifold. Their experiments show that these geometry-aware approaches significantly outperform conventional methods, suggesting a new way to model natural images effectively.

zh.title: 几何感知模型提升自然图像生成
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了自然图像的几何特性，发现语义信息主要编码在方向分量中，而范数分量可以通过全局平均来近似。我们提出了球面最优传输流匹配（SOT-CFM）和球面流匹配（SFM）方法，这些方法利用角度距离并直接在流形上约束动态。实验结果表明，这些基于几何的模型在自然图像生成方面的表现优于传统的欧几里得方法。最终，这项工作为黎曼流形建模与自然图像生成之间架起了桥梁。

papers.title: Channel-wise Vector Quantization
papers.abstract: Abstract Channel-wise Vector Quantization replaces patch-wise tokens with channel-wise tokens in image tokenization, enabling a next-channel prediction framework that generates images by sequentially refining visual details.  AI-generated summary We present Channel-wise Vector Quantization (CVQ), a novel image tokenization paradigm that replaces patch-wise tokens with channel-wise tokens. Unlike conventional vector quantization, which assigns a discrete token to each patch feature vector, CVQ quantizes each channel of the feature map. This formulation represents an image as discrete levels of visual details, rather than as a grid of spatial patches. Based on CVQ, we introduce a new visual autoregressive framework with "next-channel prediction". Instead of rendering images patch by patch in raster order, our Channel-wise Autoregressive (CAR) model predicts image channels sequentially, producing progressively enriched visual details. Specifically, it first sketches global structure and then refines fine-grained attributes, akin to a human artist's workflow. Empirically, we show that: (1) CVQ achieves 100% codebook utilization with a 16K+ codebook size without any bells and whistles, and substantially improves reconstruction quality over conventional VQ; and (2) CAR attains a DPG score of 86.7 and a GenEval score of 0.79, demonstrating strong effectiveness for text-to-image generation.
affiliations: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: От глобального контура к деталям: канально-ориентированная квантизация для аутарегрессивной генерации изображений
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новая парадигма квантизации изображений под названием Channel-wise Vector Quantization (CVQ), которая заменяет токены на основе патчей на токены на основе каналов признаков. На основе CVQ авторы представляют автарегрессивную модель CAR, которая генерирует изображения путём последовательного предсказания каналов, постепенно уточняя визуальные детали от общей структуры к мелким деталям. Предложенный подход достигает 100% использования кодовой книги с размером 16K+ и демонстрирует высокую эффективность в задаче генерации изображений по текстовому описанию. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что CVQ превосходит традиционное векторное квантизирование по качеству реконструкции, а модель CAR достигает конкурентоспособных показателей DPG и GenEval.

en.title: Revolutionizing Image Generation with Channel-wise Tokens
en.desc: This paper introduces Channel-wise Vector Quantization (CVQ), a new method for image tokenization that focuses on channel-wise tokens instead of traditional patch-wise tokens. By quantizing each channel of the feature map, CVQ allows for a more detailed representation of images, capturing visual details in a more structured way. The authors also present a Channel-wise Autoregressive (CAR) model that predicts image channels sequentially, enhancing the image generation process by refining details progressively. The results show that CVQ significantly improves image reconstruction quality and the CAR model performs well in text-to-image generation tasks.

zh.title: 通道向量量化：图像生成的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的图像标记化方法，称为通道向量量化（CVQ），它用通道级别的标记替代了传统的块级标记。CVQ通过对特征图的每个通道进行量化，能够将图像表示为离散的视觉细节层次，而不是空间块的网格。基于CVQ，我们引入了一种新的视觉自回归框架，称为通道自回归（CAR），它通过顺序预测图像通道来生成图像，逐步丰富视觉细节。实验结果表明，CVQ在重建质量上显著优于传统的向量量化方法，而CAR在文本到图像生成任务中表现出色。

papers.title: MetaphorVU: Towards Metaphorical Video Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract Current multimodal large language models struggle with metaphorical video understanding due to poor cross-domain mapping, prompting the development of a new benchmark and enhancement framework.  AI-generated summary Metaphorical videos are prevalent across various real-world scenarios to convey complex ideas, and understanding them typically requires high-order cognitive capabilities. The lack of systematic studies on metaphorical video understanding not only constrains the real-world applicability of MLLMs but also impedes the thorough assessment of their high-order cognitive capabilities. To bridge this gap, we propose MetaphorVU-Bench, the first systematic and comprehensive benchmark dedicated to metaphorical video understanding. Through experiments, we find current MLLMs struggle with accurate metaphorical video understanding, lagging far behind human level, primarily due to defective cross-domain mapping. Motivated by this finding, we construct a metaphor knowledge graph as mapping augmentation and propose MetaphorBoost, an inference-time enhancement framework achieving consistent performance improvement. Our benchmark, analysis, and method provide useful insights and a foundation for future research on advancing MLLMs.
affiliations: Chinese Information Processing Laboratory, Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Мосты смыслов: от видео метафор к глубокому пониманию AI
ru.desc: В статье представлена первая комплексная база данных MetaphorVU-Bench для оценки понимания метафорических видео многомодальными языковыми моделями. Авторы показали, что текущие MLLM плохо справляются с интерпретацией метафор в видеоконтенте из-за неэффективного кросс-доменного отображения смыслов. Для решения этой проблемы предложена система MetaphorBoost, которая использует граф знаний о метафорах для улучшения качества понимания на этапе инференса. Исследование демонстрирует критическую важность развития высокоуровневых когнитивных способностей больших языковых моделей для работы с абстрактными видеоконтентом.

en.title: Enhancing Metaphorical Video Understanding in MLLMs
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in understanding metaphorical videos, which require advanced cognitive skills. The authors introduce MetaphorVU-Bench, a new benchmark specifically designed to evaluate metaphorical video comprehension. They discover that current MLLMs perform poorly in this area due to inadequate cross-domain mapping. To improve performance, they propose MetaphorBoost, an enhancement framework that utilizes a metaphor knowledge graph to aid in better understanding during inference.

zh.title: 提升隐喻视频理解的基准与框架
zh.desc: 当前的多模态大型语言模型在理解隐喻视频方面存在困难，主要是由于跨领域映射不佳。隐喻视频在现实场景中广泛存在，用于传达复杂的思想，理解这些视频通常需要较高的认知能力。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了MetaphorVU-Bench，这是第一个专门针对隐喻视频理解的系统性基准。我们还构建了隐喻知识图谱作为映射增强，并提出了MetaphorBoost框架，以提高推理时的性能。

papers.title: Helix4D: Complex 4D Mesh Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Helix4D enables high-quality dynamic mesh generation by adapting Trellis2's frame-local attention across frames and extending 3D positional encoding with 4D temporal information.  AI-generated summary Current video-to-4D methods struggle with complex topology changes, transparent materials, thin structures, and inner surfaces. We present Helix4D, a dynamic mesh generation framework by inheriting the expressive representation of Trellis2, adapting it from image-to-3D to video-conditioned 4D generation. Our design arises from two key questions: (a) how to enable Trellis2's frame-local attention to share information across frames while preserving its pretrained quality on rare cases such as transparent objects and inner surfaces, and (b) how to inject temporal information into a purely 3D positional encoding without breaking pretrained capabilities. We address (a) with a sliding-window cross-frame attention and anchor on the first frame. The first frame is generated by the base Trellis2 model and injected into our model, letting it inherit Trellis2's quality in rare cases through cross-frame attention. We address (b) with a 4D temporal encoding that repurposes redundant low-frequency spatial RoPE bands for time, extending the encoding from 3D with no additional parameters. Extensive experiments show the effectiveness of Helix4D for high-quality dynamic mesh generation on ActionBench and our own challenging complex dynamics set.

ru.title: Видео в 4D: расширение пространственной архитектуры на временное измерение
ru.desc: Helix4D — это фреймворк для генерации динамических сеток из видео, который адаптирует архитектуру Trellis2 для работы с временным измерением. Основная идея заключается в использовании кросс-фреймовой внимания со скользящим окном, которая позволяет моделям обмениваться информацией между кадрами видео, сохраняя при этом способность исходной модели корректно обрабатывать сложные случаи вроде прозрачных объектов. Для внедрения временной информации авторы предложили 4D позиционное кодирование, которое переиспользует избыточные низкочастотные компоненты пространственного RoPE без добавления параметров. Экспериментальная оценка показывает эффективность подхода при генерации высококачественных динамических сеток с корректной обработкой сложной топологии и тонких структур.

en.title: Revolutionizing Dynamic Mesh Generation with Helix4D
en.desc: Helix4D is a framework designed for generating high-quality dynamic meshes from video data by building on the capabilities of Trellis2. It enhances the original model's frame-local attention mechanism to allow information sharing across frames, which is crucial for handling complex changes in topology and materials. The framework also introduces a novel 4D temporal encoding that integrates time information into the existing 3D positional encoding without increasing the model's parameters. Through extensive testing, Helix4D demonstrates superior performance in generating dynamic meshes, particularly in challenging scenarios involving transparent materials and intricate structures.

zh.title: Helix4D：高质量动态网格生成的新突破
zh.desc: Helix4D 是一个动态网格生成框架，能够高质量地处理视频到四维的转换。它通过继承 Trellis2 的表达能力，并在此基础上适应帧局部注意力机制，解决了复杂拓扑变化和透明材料等问题。该框架采用滑动窗口跨帧注意力，确保在生成过程中保留预训练模型的质量。通过引入四维时间编码，Helix4D 实现了在不增加额外参数的情况下，增强了三维位置编码的能力。

papers.title: Towards Customized Multimodal Role-Play
papers.abstract: Abstract A new task and dataset for customized multimodal role-play is introduced, along with a unified model framework that enables consistent character customization across text and image modalities using few-shot learning.  AI-generated summary Unified multimodal understanding and generation models enable richer human-AI interaction. Yet jointly customizing a character's persona, dialogue style, and visual identity while maintaining output consistency across modalities remains largely unexplored. To mitigate this gap, we introduce a new task, Customized Multimodal Role-Play (CMRP). We construct the RoleScape-20 dataset comprising 20 characters, including training and evaluation data that cover persona, stylistic descriptions, visual/expressive cues, and text-image interactions. Building on a unified model, we devise UniCharacter, a two-stage training framework containing Unified Supervised Finetuning (Unified-SFT) and character-specific group relative policy optimization (Character-GRPO). Given only 10 images plus corresponding interaction examples, the model acquires the target character and exhibits coherent persona, style, and visual identity in both generated text and images. This process takes about 100 GPU hours. Experiments on the RoleScape-20 dataset show that the proposed method substantially outperforms prior approaches. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of our cross-modal consistency design and few-shot customization strategy. We argue that CMRP, coupled with unified modeling, provides a basis for next-generation characterful and immersive interactive agents.

ru.title: Единая мультимодальная модель для кастомизации персонажей с согласованностью текста и изображений
ru.desc: В работе предложена новая задача Customized Multimodal Role-Play (CMRP) и датасет RoleScape-20 для создания персонажей с согласованной личностью, стилем диалога и визуальной идентичностью. Авторы разработали модель UniCharacter на основе unified архитектуры, которая использует двухэтапный процесс обучения: Unified-SFT и Character-GRPO для fine-tuning персонажей. Метод позволяет за счёт few-shot learning (всего 10 изображений и примеров взаимодействия) обучить модель генерировать согласованные текстовые и визуальные выходы с сохранением характера персонажа. Экспериментальные результаты показывают существенное улучшение по сравнению с предыдущими подходами, подтверждая эффективность кросс-модальной согласованности и стратегии few-shot customization.

en.title: Empowering AI with Customized Multimodal Role-Play
en.desc: This paper introduces a new task called Customized Multimodal Role-Play (CMRP) and a dataset named RoleScape-20, which includes 20 characters with detailed persona and visual cues. The authors present a unified model framework called UniCharacter that allows for consistent character customization across text and images using few-shot learning techniques. The model is trained in two stages, utilizing Unified Supervised Finetuning and character-specific optimization to ensure coherent persona and style in generated outputs. Experimental results demonstrate that this approach significantly improves upon previous methods, paving the way for more engaging human-AI interactions.

zh.title: 定制化多模态角色扮演的新突破
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新的任务和数据集，旨在实现定制化的多模态角色扮演。我们构建了RoleScape-20数据集，包含20个角色的个性、风格描述和视觉线索。通过统一模型框架UniCharacter，模型能够在仅有10张图像和相应的交互示例的情况下，学习目标角色的一致性个性和风格。实验结果表明，该方法在角色定制和跨模态一致性方面显著优于之前的方法。

papers.title: Reinforcing Few-step Generators via Reward-Tilted Distribution Matching
papers.abstract: Abstract RTDMD is a two-stage framework that combines distribution matching distillation with reward-guided reinforcement learning to improve few-step image generation alignment with human preferences.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in few-step diffusion distillation have enabled efficient image generation, yet aligning these models with human preferences remains challenging. We propose Reward-Tilted Distribution Matching Distillation (RTDMD), a two-stage framework that unifies distribution matching distillation with reward-guided reinforcement learning for few-step flow generators. We show that minimizing the KL divergence to a reward-tilted teacher distribution naturally decomposes into a distribution matching term and a reward maximization term. In the first stage, we introduce Ambient-Consistent Distribution Matching Distillation (AC-DMD), which performs subinterval-wise distribution matching and augments the fake score objective with a consistency regularizer to help the fake score model track the shifting generator distribution under limited updates. In the second stage, we jointly optimize both terms: for the reward maximization term, we derive a hybrid policy gradient that combines a GRPO-style estimator for the stochastic intermediate transitions with direct reward backpropagation through the deterministic final step, and further introduce step-subset GRPO (SubGRPO) to reduce variance. Experiments on SD3, SD3.5, and FLUX.2 demonstrate that RTDMD establishes new state-of-the-art results across preference, aesthetic, and compositional metrics with only 4 inference steps, outperforming previous few-step text-to-image generation methods. Code and models are available at https://github.com/Harahan/RTDMD.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Вознаграждение в сердце дистилляции: быстрая генерация в согласии с человеком
ru.desc: RTDMD — это двухэтапная система для улучшения генерации изображений за несколько шагов путём согласования модели с предпочтениями человека. На первом этапе используется дистилляция распределения с консистентностью (AC-DMD), которая выполняет поэтапное сопоставление распределений между учителем и студентом. На втором этапе применяется комбинированный градиентный спуск политики, объединяющий оценивание вознаграждения с оптимизацией потока. Метод показывает передовые результаты на моделях SD3, SD3.5 и FLUX.2, достигая высокого качества всего за 4 шага генерации.

en.title: Aligning AI Image Generation with Human Preferences Efficiently
en.desc: The paper presents RTDMD, a novel two-stage framework designed to enhance few-step image generation by aligning it more closely with human preferences. It integrates distribution matching distillation with reward-guided reinforcement learning, addressing the challenge of aligning AI-generated images with human expectations. The first stage, AC-DMD, focuses on distribution matching while maintaining consistency in the generated images, and the second stage optimizes both distribution matching and reward maximization using a hybrid policy gradient approach. Experimental results show that RTDMD achieves state-of-the-art performance in various metrics with only four inference steps, surpassing previous methods in text-to-image generation.

zh.title: 提升图像生成与人类偏好的对齐
zh.desc: RTDMD是一种两阶段框架，结合了分布匹配蒸馏和基于奖励的强化学习，以提高少步图像生成与人类偏好的对齐。该方法通过最小化与奖励倾斜教师分布的KL散度，将目标分解为分布匹配项和奖励最大化项。在第一阶段，采用环境一致性分布匹配蒸馏（AC-DMD），通过子区间分布匹配和一致性正则化来帮助模型跟踪生成器分布的变化。在第二阶段，联合优化这两个项，使用混合策略梯度方法来减少方差，从而在仅需4步推理的情况下，RTDMD在多个指标上实现了新的最先进结果。

papers.title: SemBridge: Language Transfer in Sparse Encoders via Multilingual Semantic Bridges
papers.abstract: Abstract SemBridge enhances cross-lingual sparse encoder adaptation by using multilingual bridge models to establish semantic alignments and improve retrieval performance across multiple languages.  AI-generated summary Sparse encoders offer high-precision retrieval by representing term importance within a vocabulary space, yet their English-centric structures pose a critical impediment to language transfer for non-English languages. To overcome this structural limitation, we propose SemBridge, a novel embedding initialization method designed for cross-lingual adaptation in sparse encoders by leveraging multilingual bridge models. SemBridge establishes semantic alignments between source and target vocabularies using multilingual dense embeddings as a bridge. Rather than directly relying on all source tokens, SemBridge selects a small set of semantically related source-language tokens and uses them to initialize each target-language token, effectively filtering out semantic noise and reconstructing target tokens as precise linear combinations of core synonyms. This accelerates convergence during fine-tuning and improves training efficiency. Extensive experiments across five languages and four sparse architectures demonstrate that SemBridge achieves superior zero-shot retrieval performance and consistently improves retrieval performance after fine-tuning compared to existing baselines. These results validate SemBridge as a practical solution for deploying high-performance sparse retrieval systems in diverse linguistic environments.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Korea University

ru.title: Семантический мост для кросс-лингвального поиска через разреженные энкодеры
