ru.desc: SemBridge — это метод инициализации эмбеддингов для адаптации разреженных энкодеров на кросс-лингвальной основе. Метод использует многоязычные плотные модели в качестве моста для установления семантических выравниваний между словарями исходного и целевого языков. Вместо использования всех исходных токенов, SemBridge отбирает небольшое подмножество семантически связанных токенов и инициализирует ими целевые токены, что снижает семантический шум. Экспериментальная оценка на пяти языках показывает, что метод достигает лучшей производительности поиска как в нулевом приближении, так и после тонкой настройки.

en.title: Bridging Languages for Better Sparse Retrieval
en.desc: SemBridge is a method that improves how sparse encoders adapt to different languages by using multilingual bridge models. It addresses the problem of English-centric structures in sparse encoders, which makes it hard for non-English languages to perform well. By establishing semantic alignments between vocabularies, SemBridge filters out irrelevant information and initializes target-language tokens with relevant source-language tokens. This leads to faster training and better retrieval performance across multiple languages, proving its effectiveness in diverse linguistic settings.

zh.title: SemBridge：跨语言检索的新桥梁
zh.desc: SemBridge是一种新颖的嵌入初始化方法，旨在通过多语言桥接模型来增强跨语言稀疏编码器的适应性。它通过建立源语言和目标语言词汇之间的语义对齐，来提高多语言检索性能。SemBridge选择一小部分与目标语言相关的源语言词汇，利用这些词汇来初始化目标语言词汇，从而有效过滤语义噪声。实验结果表明，SemBridge在五种语言和四种稀疏架构中表现出色，显著提升了零样本检索性能和微调后的检索效果。

papers.title: Faithfulness Metrics Don't Measure Faithfulness: A Meta-Evaluation with Ground Truth
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers created a benchmark with 3,066 labeled chains of thought examples across 13 tasks and 10 models to systematically evaluate faithfulness metrics, revealing that most metrics perform near randomly and have significant limitations in reliability and efficiency.  AI-generated summary Chains of thought (CoTs) have become central in interpreting and auditing behaviors of large language models. Yet growing evidence suggests that these traces often fail to faithfully represent the computations behind a model's predictions. Several faithfulness metrics have been proposed, but whether they indeed measure faithfulness remains unknown. Answering this requires ground-truth labels, which are hard to obtain since internal computations are not directly observable. Consequently, most works proposing metrics report only absolute scores or comparisons to prior metrics, and the few existing benchmarks rely on proxies like plausibility or importance, properties orthogonal to faithfulness that can mislead about whether a CoT can be trusted. We address this challenge by constructing tasks whose outputs reveal which intermediate computations must have produced them, and developing an automated labeling pipeline that yields ground-truth faithfulness labels at both the step and CoT level. Building on this methodology, we present BonaFide, a benchmark of 3,066 labeled CoTs across 13 tasks and 10 models, and use it to conduct the first systematic evaluation of prominent faithfulness metrics. Our experiments show that most metrics perform near chance, exhibit strong prediction biases and degrade on longer CoTs. The best metric reaches only 0.70 AUROC at the CoT level while another reaches 0.59 at the step level, with neither transferring across settings, while entailing prohibitively high computational cost. Our results expose fundamental gaps in current faithfulness evaluation and call for the development of more reliable and efficient metrics.

ru.title: Верность рассуждений LLM: миф или реальность?
ru.desc: Исследователи создали бенчмарк BonaFide с 3066 размеченными примерами цепочек рассуждений (Chain of Thought) на 13 задачах и 10 моделях для систематической оценки метрик верности. Работа показывает, что большинство существующих метрик верности работают практически случайно и не способны надежно определить, действительно ли промежуточные шаги в цепочке рассуждений отражают вычисления модели. Авторы разработали методологию автоматического создания ground-truth меток верности путем конструирования задач, выходы которых однозначно раскрывают необходимые промежуточные вычисления. Результаты выявили критические пробелы в текущих подходах к оценке верности и подчеркнули необходимость разработки более надежных и эффективных метрик.

en.title: BonaFide: A New Benchmark for Evaluating Faithfulness in AI Models
en.desc: This paper introduces a benchmark called BonaFide, which includes 3,066 labeled chains of thought (CoTs) across 13 tasks and 10 models to evaluate faithfulness metrics in large language models. The study reveals that many existing faithfulness metrics perform poorly, often yielding results that are close to random chance and showing significant biases. The authors highlight the challenges in measuring faithfulness due to the lack of ground-truth labels, as internal model computations are not directly observable. Their findings indicate a critical need for more reliable and efficient metrics to assess the faithfulness of CoTs in AI models.

zh.title: 揭示忠实度评估的缺陷，呼唤新指标的诞生
zh.desc: 研究人员创建了一个基准，包含3066个标记的思维链示例，涵盖13个任务和10个模型，以系统评估忠实度指标。结果显示，大多数指标的表现接近随机，且在可靠性和效率上存在显著局限性。尽管已有多个忠实度指标被提出，但它们是否真正测量忠实度仍然未知。我们的研究揭示了当前忠实度评估的根本缺陷，并呼吁开发更可靠和高效的指标。

papers.title: SEAL: Synergistic Co-Evolution of Agents and Learning Environments
papers.abstract: Abstract SEAL is a closed-loop co-evolution framework that simultaneously adapts both agent policies and training environments to improve interactive tool-use capabilities in large language models.  AI-generated summary Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly improved through interaction, yet most self-evolution methods adapt either the policy or the learning environment in isolation. We identify this structural gap as Agent-Environment Misalignment: the agent's capability frontier changes during training, while the environment that provides supervision remains static or only weakly coupled to the agent's revealed failures. We propose SEAL, a closed-loop co-evolution framework for interactive tool-use agents. SEAL collects on-policy trajectories under executable verification, diagnoses failed rollouts into turn-level failure labels, and uses these diagnoses as a shared signal for both environment-side adaptation and model-side policy optimization. The environment evolves its training-time learning interface by exposing clearer tool affordance cues, constraint information, and recovery-oriented feedback, while the policy is updated with diagnosis-guided advantage reweighting. Extensive experiments across in-distribution and out-of-distribution multi-turn tool-use evaluations show that SEAL improves low-resource agent learning: with only 400 training samples, it yields +8.25 to +26.25 average-point gains across three backbones and exhibits positive out-of-distribution transfer. These results demonstrate the value of jointly adapting the learner and its training-time learning substrate for robust self-improving LLM agents.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Совместная эволюция агента и окружения для самосовершенствующихся LLM
ru.desc: SEAL — это фреймворк совместной эволюции агента и окружения, который одновременно оптимизирует политику языковой модели и адаптирует обучающую среду для улучшения навыков использования инструментов. Авторы выявили проблему рассогласования агента и окружения: способности агента развиваются во время обучения, а обучающая среда остаётся статичной или слабо связана с выявленными ошибками. SEAL собирает траектории на основе текущей политики с верификацией, диагностирует ошибки и использует эту информацию для одновременного улучшения как среды обучения, так и политики модели через перевзвешивание преимуществ. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительные улучшения в обучении с ограниченными данными: всего 400 образцов дают прирост в 8-26 пунктов, а метод хорошо работает на данных вне распределения обучающего набора.

en.title: Co-evolving Agents and Environments for Enhanced Learning
en.desc: SEAL is a novel framework designed to enhance the capabilities of large language model (LLM) agents by simultaneously evolving both their policies and the training environments. It addresses the issue of Agent-Environment Misalignment, where the agent's abilities change during training while the environment remains static. By collecting on-policy trajectories and diagnosing failures, SEAL provides feedback that helps both the agent and the environment adapt together. Experimental results show that SEAL significantly improves learning efficiency and performance, even with limited training data, demonstrating the benefits of this co-evolution approach.

zh.title: 共同进化，提升智能体能力
zh.desc: SEAL是一个闭环共进化框架，旨在同时调整智能体的策略和训练环境，以提高大型语言模型的交互工具使用能力。传统的自我进化方法通常只单独适应策略或学习环境，导致智能体与环境之间存在不匹配。SEAL通过收集可执行验证下的策略轨迹，诊断失败的回合并生成失败标签，利用这些信息共同优化环境和模型策略。实验结果表明，SEAL在低资源条件下显著提升了智能体的学习效果，展示了联合适应学习者和训练环境的重要性。

papers.title: MemForest: An Efficient Agent Memory System with Hierarchical Temporal Indexing
papers.abstract: Abstract MemForest presents a memory framework for long-context LLM agents that improves scalability and reduces latency through parallel chunk extraction and hierarchical temporal indexing.  AI-generated summary Memory is a fundamental component for enabling long-context LLM agents, supporting persistent state across interactions through a continuous serve-and-update lifecycle. Despite substantial prior work, existing systems suffer from significant maintenance overhead due to two key limitations: coarse-grained state management and inherently sequential update pipelines. In particular, updates are often tightly coupled with LLM inference and require full-state rewrites, leading to poor scalability and growing latency as memory accumulates. To address these challenges, we present MemForest, a memory framework that reformulates agent memory as a write-efficient temporal data management problem. MemForest breaks the sequential bottleneck via parallel chunk extraction, decoupling memory construction into concurrent, independent operations. To further eliminate coarse-grained maintenance, we introduce MemTree, a hierarchical temporal index that organizes memory as time-ordered trees rather than flat global summaries. This design replaces full-state rewrites with localized per-node updates, reducing maintenance cost to the affected tree paths while naturally preserving temporally evolving states. We evaluate MemForest on two long-context memory benchmarks, LongMemEval-S and LoCoMo. On LongMemEval-S, MemForest achieves the best overall performance among stateful baselines, reaching 79.8% pass@1 accuracy while sustaining a memory construction throughput approximately 6x higher than state-of-the-art approaches including EverMemOS.

ru.title: Иерархическая память для масштабируемых LLM агентов через параллельную обработку
ru.desc: MemForest представляет фреймворк для управления памятью в долгоконтекстных LLM агентах, который решает проблемы масштабируемости и задержек. Основная идея заключается в переформатировании памяти агента как проблемы эффективного управления временными данными с параллельной обработкой. Авторы вводят MemTree — иерархический временной индекс, организующий память в виде упорядоченных по времени деревьев вместо плоских глобальных резюме. Это позволяет заменить полные переписи состояния локализованными обновлениями и достичь пропускной способности памяти в 6 раз выше, чем существующие методы.

en.title: Revolutionizing Memory Management for Long-Context LLMs
en.desc: MemForest introduces a new memory framework designed for long-context large language model (LLM) agents, enhancing their scalability and reducing latency. It addresses the limitations of existing systems by reformulating memory management as a temporal data problem, allowing for more efficient updates. The framework utilizes parallel chunk extraction to decouple memory construction from LLM inference, enabling concurrent operations. Additionally, MemTree, a hierarchical temporal index, organizes memory in a time-ordered manner, allowing for localized updates and significantly lowering maintenance costs.

zh.title: MemForest：高效的长上下文记忆管理框架
zh.desc: MemForest 是一个用于长上下文 LLM 代理的记忆框架，旨在提高可扩展性并减少延迟。它通过并行块提取和层次时间索引来优化记忆管理，解决了现有系统在状态管理和更新管道上的不足。MemForest 将代理记忆重新定义为一个高效的时间数据管理问题，采用并行操作来打破顺序瓶颈。通过引入 MemTree 层次索引，MemForest 实现了局部更新，降低了维护成本，同时保持了时间演变状态的完整性。

papers.title: Pixel-Level Pavement Distress Assessment Using Instance Segmentation
papers.abstract: Abstract A vision-based pavement distress analysis system using Mask R-CNN instance segmentation demonstrates superior performance for crack detection and quantification compared to object detection approaches, achieving high precision and recall metrics on a custom field-collected dataset.  AI-generated summary Automated pavement distress assessment requires more than image-level classification or coarse bounding box detection, demanding precise localization of thin, branching, and irregular cracks to achieve the geometric precision necessary for maintenance-relevant quantification. This paper presents a vision-based pavement distress analysis system based on Mask R-CNN instance segmentation and evaluates it on UWGB-StreetCrack, a custom field-collected roadway image dataset acquired with a vehicle-mounted smartphone and manually annotated with polygon labels for longitudinal cracks, transverse cracks, alligator cracks, and potholes. Five Detectron2-based Mask R-CNN backbone variants were considered under a consistent fine-tuning protocol. The best-performing model, Mask R-CNN with a ResNet-101 FPN backbone, achieved 84.23% precision, 90.04% recall, and an F1 score of 87.04% under the project-specific bounding-box matching protocol. The same model produced an aggregate predicted crack-area fraction of 2.164%, closely matching the 2.170% ground-truth crack-area fraction. To contextualize the segmentation system against a detector-oriented alternative, a CSPDarknet53-based YOLO detector was also adapted and retrained on the dataset, reaching 27.5% precision and 20.7% recall on the validation protocol. The results show that instance segmentation is a practical direction for field pavement imagery and aggregate crack-area estimation, while also exposing open challenges in annotation consistency, class imbalance, confounder rejection, and mask-level benchmarking.

ru.title: Сегментация экземпляров вместо детекции для точного анализа трещин на асфальте
ru.desc: В статье представлена система анализа дефектов дорожного покрытия на основе нейросетевой архитектуры Mask R-CNN, которая выполняет сегментацию экземпляров трещин на дорогах. Система протестирована на собственном наборе данных UWGB-StreetCrack, содержащем размеченные вручную изображения различных типов трещин и выбоин. Лучшая модель на основе ResNet-101 FPN достигла высоких показателей точности (84.23%) и полноты (90.04%), что значительно превосходит результаты традиционных детекторов объектов. Применение семантической сегментации позволяет не только локализовать тонкие и ветвящиеся трещины, но и точно оценить общую площадь повреждений для целей планирования дорожного обслуживания.

en.title: Precision in Pavement Distress Detection with Mask R-CNN
en.desc: This paper introduces a vision-based system for analyzing pavement distress using Mask R-CNN, which excels in detecting and quantifying cracks compared to traditional object detection methods. The system was tested on a custom dataset called UWGB-StreetCrack, which includes various types of pavement cracks and potholes, annotated with precise polygon labels. The best model achieved impressive metrics, including 84.23% precision and 90.04% recall, demonstrating its effectiveness in accurately localizing and quantifying pavement damage. The study highlights the advantages of instance segmentation for detailed analysis while addressing challenges like annotation consistency and class imbalance.

zh.title: 实例分割提升路面病害检测精度
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种基于Mask R-CNN实例分割的路面病害分析系统，能够高效检测和量化路面裂缝。与传统的目标检测方法相比，该系统在自定义数据集上表现出更高的精度和召回率。研究中使用了手动标注的多边形标签，针对不同类型的裂缝进行分析，确保了几何精度。结果表明，实例分割在路面图像分析和裂缝面积估计方面具有实际应用价值，同时也暴露了标注一致性和类别不平衡等挑战。

papers.title: ClaimDiff-RL: Fine-Grained Caption Reinforcement Learning through Visual Claim Comparison
papers.abstract: Abstract ClaimDiff-RL addresses the reward granularity issue in long-form image captioning by using reference-conditioned atomic claim differences as reward units, enabling separate measurement and tuning of hallucination and omission errors.  AI-generated summary Long-form image captioning exposes a reward granularity problem in RL: captions are judged as whole sequences, while the important errors occur at the level of individual visual claims. A good dense caption should be both faithful and informative, avoiding hallucination without omitting salient details. Yet pairwise preferences, reference-based metrics, and holistic scalar rewards compress these local errors into a single sequence-level signal, obscuring the tradeoff between factuality and coverage. We introduce ClaimDiff-RL, a framework that uses reference-conditioned atomic claim differences as the reward unit for caption RL. Given an image, an actor caption, and a reference caption, a multimodal judge enumerates visually grounded differences, verifies each difference against the image, assigns open-vocabulary error types and severity levels, and produces per-difference statistics for reward composition. This makes hallucinated claims and omitted salient facts separately measurable and tunable. Experiments show that holistic scalar rewards can reduce hallucination by increasing missing facts, while ClaimDiff-RL exposes this faithfulness and coverage tradeoff and enables more balanced operating points. On a 160-image human-labeled diagnostic benchmark, public captioning benchmarks, and VQA benchmarks, ClaimDiff-RL improves the hallucination--missing-fact balance, preserves general capability, and even surpasses Gemini-3-Pro-Preview on several fine-grained Capability dimensions such as object counting, spatial relations, and scene recognition. These results suggest that typed, verifiable claim differences are an effective reward unit for fine-grained and diagnosable caption RL.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Атомарные различия в утверждениях как единицы управляемого обучения подписей к изображениям
ru.desc: ClaimDiff-RL решает проблему зернистости награды в задаче создания развёрнутых подписей к изображениям, используя атомарные различия в утверждениях в качестве единиц обучения с подкреплением. Вместо оценки всей последовательности целиком, система выявляет и проверяет отдельные визуальные утверждения, классифицируя ошибки как галлюцинации или пропуски релевантной информации. Мультимодальный судья анализирует различия между сгенерированной и эталонной подписями, присваивая каждой ошибке тип и уровень серьёзности для точной композиции награды. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что такой подход достигает лучшего баланса между точностью и полнотой, превосходя базовые модели на задачах счёта объектов, пространственных отношений и распознавания сцен.

en.title: Refining Rewards for Better Image Captions
en.desc: ClaimDiff-RL tackles the challenge of reward granularity in long-form image captioning by introducing reference-conditioned atomic claim differences as reward units. This approach allows for the separate evaluation and adjustment of hallucination and omission errors in generated captions. By focusing on individual visual claims rather than treating captions as whole sequences, it provides a clearer understanding of the tradeoff between factual accuracy and coverage. Experiments demonstrate that ClaimDiff-RL enhances the balance between hallucinated claims and omitted facts, leading to improved performance on various benchmarks.

zh.title: 精细化奖励单位，提升图像描述质量
zh.desc: ClaimDiff-RL 解决了长文本图像描述中的奖励粒度问题，通过使用参考条件的原子声明差异作为奖励单位，使得对幻觉和遗漏错误的测量和调整可以分开进行。长文本图像描述的奖励粒度问题在于，描述是作为整体序列来评判的，而重要的错误发生在单个视觉声明的层面。一个好的密集描述应该既真实又信息丰富，避免幻觉而不遗漏重要细节。ClaimDiff-RL 框架通过对每个声明差异进行验证和统计，使得幻觉声明和遗漏事实可以被单独测量和调整，从而改善了描述的准确性和覆盖率。
